g4_revenue_trend.description: Revenue held roughly flat around $25.9B across 2017-2019. The business was mature, but there was no visible structural decline in the snack portfolio by late 2020.

g5_gross_margin_trend.description: Gross margin improved from about 38.7% in 2017 to 39.9% in 2018 and 40.0% in 2019. Margin quality was stable to better, not worse.

g7_moat.description: Mondelez had a real brand and distribution moat in global snacks, with scaled shelf presence and consumer habit. The issue was not moat absence, but whether a mature branded-food asset at that price still met a Buffett-style return hurdle.

owner_earnings_note.summary: The modest g2 miss does not signal a broken business; it reflects a mature branded-food company whose cash conversion was fine but not exceptional after capex and working capital.
owner_earnings_note.maintenance_vs_growth_view: Capex was not wildly expansionary, so this is closer to a genuine mediocre conversion signal than a Wal-Mart-style reinvestment distortion.
owner_earnings_note.decision_impact: The g2 fail is real enough to keep on the record, and it combines with a low earnings yield to hold the conclusion at PASS.

quality_multiple_note.summary: Mondelez traded on a premium consumer-staples multiple because the market valued its global snack portfolio and defensive cash flows.
quality_multiple_note.reason_for_low_yield: The low yield reflected quality and scarcity, but also a world where many branded staples already traded at compressed expected returns.
quality_multiple_note.decision_impact: The note explains the valuation backdrop without overturning the PASS.

management_veto.summary: No management veto applied. The debate is about adequate return at the paid price, not trust in stewardship.

control_groups.conclusion: PASS — another large food company, but with much more visible franchise decay.
PASS — stable business, but without Mondelez's global snack position.

buffett_action: Berkshire reported 578,000 Mondelez shares worth about $33.8M at 2020-12-31 in its 13F-HR.
actual_result: The small position remained unchanged at 578,000 shares through 2021-12-31 and 2022-12-31. It behaved more like a legacy or tracker position than a major Berkshire thesis.
key_insight: Mature consumer brands can be perfectly sound businesses and still fail the framework when cash conversion is merely okay and the starting earnings yield is under 4%.
